tweets.text:
The MOU is making the rounds around mainstream media. The MOU published by Bloomberg is identical to the one leaked by Mehr, which is concerning given that Iranian media outlets are more accurate than American ones. 

What a ridiculous time we live in. 

Trump is going to have a tantrum before we even “sign” the MOU. 

The fact that the US still hasn’t published the MOU tells you how unfavorable it is. 

Meanwhile, Iranian tankers are already making it past the blockade. 

This is like a house of cards in the middle of a hurricane. An unstable equilibrium that is bound to create chaos again.

tweets.text:
Here's a Saylor interview from 2023 encouraging corporations to buy Bitcoin with excess cash on their balance sheet and escape the destructive cycle of expensive acquisitions, buybacks, dividends, & debt.

The irony is, Strategy is now trapped in that dividends & debt cycle, and rather than adding a company or other assets via acquisitions that generates revenue, they've leveraged their debt to buy an asset that they're down ~$10 billion today.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: #Bitcoin offers corporations an innovative strategy to preserve their capital and create shareholder value by leveraging their balance sheets with $BTC, providing an escape from the destructive cycle of expensive acquisitions, buybacks, dividends, & debt. https://t.co/n8mPApnbiv

lol man I wish I was being paid for that but I just fully believe there’s a fundamental issue I was blind to for way too long that I believe is worth sharing

I’ve posted multiple times about how I was wrong to be as bullish as I was with short term price action and didn’t start to wake up until seeing some of the recent lies by Saylor

I’m still bullish as hell on Bitcoin and crypto as a whole, but this is a Ponzi scheme worth calling out
